free_text: Production of Sn and Sb isotopes in high-energy
neutron-induced fission of natU
K-130 cyclotron
30 MeV protons on a 6 mm-thick Be converter
A white neutron spectrum extending to about 30 MeV.
The average neutron energy weighted with the fission
cross section of 238U, accounting for (98+/-1)% of
the fission events, is (12.4+/-8.8)MeV.
Neutron spectrum characterization
Two natU foils (1x5 cm2 and 15 mg/cm2 thick) attached
to a thin Al support and placed at distance of 2.5 cm
from the back of the neutron source.
IGISOL-4 facility in JYFL-ACCLAB.
The fission fragments were stopped in He gas,
extracted from the ion guide gas cell, accelerated to
24 keV, and separated online based on their
charge-over-mass ratio using a dipole magnet with a
mass resolving power M/deltaM~500.
Fission products accumulated on a tape for
60s to 15 min were measured for 7200 sec (A=128-132)
Coincidences between gamma-rays and electrons
from the beta-decay of the fission products
70% coaxial p-type HPGe detector
Plastic scintillator to detect electrons from
beta- decay.

yields in ions/sec
The independent yield determined by subtracting the
contribution of the cumulative fission yields of Sn
from the measured cumulative fission yields of Sb.
